title: Products & Services
lede: Build your catalog once and quote in seconds forever after. Products and Services are the reusable building blocks behind every quote, invoice, and job in iMash — complete with prices, costs, live profit margins, and automatic inventory tracking.

steps.title: Open the Products catalog
steps.content:
Everything you sell that has a price tag — parts, equipment, plans, digital goods — lives in the Products catalog.

  1. In the top navigation, click CRM, then click the Products tab (or go directly to /pipeline/products).
  2. You'll see your full catalog in a table. Each row shows the essentials at a glance:
  • Product Name — click it to open the product.
  • SKU — your internal code, shown in monospace so it's easy to copy.
  • Type — a colored pill: physical, digital, service, or subscription.
  • Category and Tags — how you organize the catalog.
  • Price and Cost — what you charge and what it costs you.
  • Margin — calculated automatically from Price and Cost, color-coded: green for 50%+, blue for 30%+, amber below that. Instant profit visibility on every item.
  • Stock — the live quantity for inventory-tracked products, with Low Stock and Out of Stock warnings (products without tracking show N/A).
  • Status — Active or Inactive.
  • Actions — the eye icon (View) opens the product, the pencil icon (Edit) opens the edit form.

images.alt: Products list showing the catalog table with type pills, prices and action icons
images.caption: The Products catalog — every item with its type, price, and per-row View / Edit actions

images.alt: Products list showing the catalog table with type pills, prices and action icons
images.caption: The Products catalog — every item with its type, price, and per-row View / Edit actions

steps.title: Search and filter the catalog
steps.content:
Even a catalog with thousands of items stays searchable:

  1. Click Show Filters in the top-right (next to Create Product). A filter bar appears above the table.
  2. Type in Search products... and press Enter — the search covers name, SKU, category, and description across the whole catalog, not just the visible page.
  3. Narrow further with the dropdowns: Filter by category, Filter by type, Filter by status (Active/Inactive), and Filter by owner (which team member owns the record).
  • Column filters — the small funnel icons on the Type and Status column headers filter right from the table.
  • Sorting — click the Price column header to sort by price; click again to flip the direction.
  • Pagination — the footer shows Total N products; use the page-size selector to show more rows per page.
💡 The category dropdowns are only as good as your category list — define your product and service categories in CRM Settings first, and picking the right one at creation time takes one click.

images.alt: Products page with the filter bar open showing search, category, type, status and owner filters
images.caption: Click Show Filters to reveal search plus category, type, status, and owner filters

images.alt: Products page with the filter bar open showing search, category, type, status and owner filters
images.caption: Click Show Filters to reveal search plus category, type, status, and owner filters

steps.title: Create your first product
steps.content:
1. Click Create Product in the top-right of the Products list. The Create New Product form opens.
  2. Enter the Product Name — the only required field.
  3. Fill in the rest to make the product truly useful:
  • SKU/Code — your internal part number or code, searchable everywhere.
  • Category — pick from the categories you defined in CRM Settings.
  • Description — shown when browsing the catalog and useful on documents.
  • Product Type — Physical Product, Digital Product, Service, or Subscription. The type changes which extra fields appear (see the next two steps).
  • Status — the Active/Inactive switch. Inactive products stay in your records but stop appearing as choices.
  • Base Price — what you charge. The currency selector (default USD) sits right beside it.
  • Cost — what it costs you. Optional, but fill it in: it powers the automatic Margin column so you always know which items make you money.
  • Tags — type a tag and press Enter; add as many as you like.
  • Product Image — click Upload to attach a photo.
  1. Click Create Product in the top-right of the form. To back out without saving, click Cancel.

images.alt: Create New Product form with name, SKU, category, description, type, status, price, cost, tags and image fields
images.caption: The Create New Product form — only Product Name is required

images.alt: Create New Product form with name, SKU, category, description, type, status, price, cost, tags and image fields
images.caption: The Create New Product form — only Product Name is required

steps.title: Turn on inventory tracking
steps.content:
For Physical and Digital products, iMash can count your stock for you.

  1. In the product form, switch on Track Inventory. Two new fields appear.
  2. Enter the Quantity Available — how many units you have right now.
  3. Set a Low Stock Threshold — when the quantity drops to this number or below, the product's Stock pill in the list turns into an amber Low Stock warning; at zero it becomes a red Out of Stock pill.
💡 Here's the magic: when an invoice that includes this product is paid, the quantity decreases automatically. Your stock count keeps itself honest — no manual bookkeeping after every sale.

images.alt: Product form with Track Inventory enabled showing Quantity Available and Low Stock Threshold fields
images.caption: Track Inventory on — set the current quantity and a low-stock warning threshold

images.alt: Product form with Track Inventory enabled showing Quantity Available and Low Stock Threshold fields
images.caption: Track Inventory on — set the current quantity and a low-stock warning threshold

steps.title: Sell recurring plans with subscription products
steps.content:
Maintenance plans, memberships, monthly retainers on autopilot — set the Product Type to Subscription and two recurring-billing fields appear:

  • Billing Cycle — how often the customer is charged: Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly, Semi-Annual, Annual, or Biennial.
  • Trial Period (Days) — an optional free trial before the first charge.

The Base Price becomes the per-cycle amount (for example $29 per month). Manage the customers signed up to these plans on the Subscriptions page.

images.alt: Product form with type set to Subscription showing Billing Cycle and Trial Period fields
images.caption: Subscription type — pick a Billing Cycle and an optional Trial Period

images.alt: Product form with type set to Subscription showing Billing Cycle and Trial Period fields
images.caption: Subscription type — pick a Billing Cycle and an optional Trial Period

steps.title: Open the Services catalog
steps.content:
Products are things; Services are work — tune-ups, installations, consulting hours, monthly retainers. They get their own catalog built for how work is priced.

  1. In the top navigation, click CRM, then the Services tab (or go to /pipeline/services).
  2. The table mirrors the Products list with service-specific columns:
  • Service Name and Code — the display name and your internal code.
  • Pricing Model — a colored pill: Hourly, Fixed, Retainer, Value-Based, or Tiered.
  • Base Rate — the price (sortable by clicking the header).
  • Est. Hours and Est. Duration — how long the work takes, in hours and calendar days.
  • Deliverables — a count of what the customer receives.
  • Status, Tags, Owner, and the View / Edit action icons — just like Products.

The Show Filters bar works the same way too: search by name, code, category, or description, and filter by category, status, and owner. The Pricing Model column has its own funnel filter.

images.alt: Services list showing services with pricing model pills, base rates and estimated hours
images.caption: The Services catalog — pricing models, rates, and time estimates at a glance

images.alt: Services list showing services with pricing model pills, base rates and estimated hours
images.caption: The Services catalog — pricing models, rates, and time estimates at a glance

steps.title: Create a service
steps.content:
1. Click Create Service in the top-right of the Services list. The Create New Service form opens.
  2. Enter the Service Name — the only required field.
  3. Complete the profile:
  • Service Code — your internal code or SKU.
  • Category — from your service categories in CRM Settings.
  • Description — what the service includes.
  • Pricing Model — see the next step for choosing the right one.
  • Status — the Active/Inactive switch.
  • Hourly Rate / Monthly Fee / Base Rate — the price field, with a currency selector. Its label changes with the pricing model you picked.
  • Estimated Hours — labor time (supports half hours, e.g. 1.5).
  • Estimated Duration (Days) — calendar time from start to finish.
  • Tags — type and press Enter.
  • Deliverables — what the customer gets (e.g. 21-point inspection report, Filter replacement). Type each item and press Enter.
  • Requirements — what you need from the customer before work starts (e.g. Clear access to outdoor unit).
  1. Click Create Service to save.
💡 Deliverables and Requirements turn a vague "service" into a crystal-clear promise — they set expectations before the job starts and prevent "I thought that was included" conversations after.

images.alt: Create New Service form with name, code, category, pricing model, rate, time estimates, deliverables and requirements
images.caption: The Create New Service form — pricing, time estimates, deliverables and requirements

images.alt: Create New Service form with name, code, category, pricing model, rate, time estimates, deliverables and requirements
images.caption: The Create New Service form — pricing, time estimates, deliverables and requirements

steps.title: Choose the right pricing model
steps.content:
Click the Pricing Model dropdown to see all five ways to price a service:

  • Hourly Rate — you bill per hour; the price field becomes Hourly Rate. Great for repairs and consulting.
  • Fixed Price — one flat price for the whole job (e.g. a $129 tune-up). The customer knows the total up front.
  • Monthly Retainer — a recurring monthly engagement; the price field becomes Monthly Fee.
  • Value-Based — priced by the outcome delivered rather than time spent.
  • Tiered Pricing — different levels at different price points (e.g. Basic / Pro / Premium packages).

The model shows as a colored pill in the Services list, so your team can tell at a glance how each service is billed.

images.alt: Pricing Model dropdown open showing Hourly Rate, Fixed Price, Monthly Retainer, Value-Based and Tiered Pricing options
images.caption: The five pricing models — the price field's label adapts to your choice

images.alt: Pricing Model dropdown open showing Hourly Rate, Fixed Price, Monthly Retainer, Value-Based and Tiered Pricing options
images.caption: The five pricing models — the price field's label adapts to your choice

steps.title: Use your catalog in quotes, invoices, and jobs
steps.content:
This is where the catalog pays off — no more retyping names and prices on every document:

  1. When building a quote or editing an invoice, go to the line-items section and click Select from Catalog (next to Add Line Item).
  2. The Select Products/Services window opens with two tabs — Products and Services — and a search box that matches by name, SKU, or code.
  3. Tick the checkbox on every item you want; your picks stack up as removable chips under the search box, and you can mix products and services in one go.
  4. Click Add N Items. Each item lands as a line item with its name and price pre-filled — then adjust quantities, discounts, and tax per line as needed.

The same catalog also feeds job line items in Jobs, so a technician's work order, the quote, and the final invoice all speak the same language.

⚠️ Only Active items are offered in the picker. Don't delete a discontinued product — flip its Status to Inactive instead, and your historical quotes and invoices keep their records intact.

images.alt: Select Products/Services window with Products and Services tabs, search box and selectable rows
images.caption: Select from Catalog — search, tick multiple items across both tabs, then Add Items

images.alt: Select Products/Services window with Products and Services tabs, search box and selectable rows
images.caption: Select from Catalog — search, tick multiple items across both tabs, then Add Items

steps.title: Keep the catalog healthy
steps.content:
A five-minute monthly habit keeps everything downstream accurate:

  • Update prices in one place — edit the product or service (pencil icon) and every future quote and invoice picks up the new price. Documents you already sent keep the price they were created with.
  • Watch the Margin column — amber margins are your cue to raise a price or renegotiate a supplier cost.
  • Trust the Stock pills — Low Stock warnings tell you what to reorder before you have to tell a customer "it's on backorder".
  • Retire with Inactive, not delete — deactivated items disappear from pickers but preserve your history.
  • Curate categories and tags — manage the category lists in CRM Settings so filtering stays fast as the catalog grows.

That's it — your catalog is now the single source of truth for everything you sell. Quote faster, invoice cleaner, and never guess a price again.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content: Questions about products, services, or inventory tracking? Reach us any time at support@imash.io — we're happy to help you structure your catalog.
